design_philosophy.core_loop: Select character → engage opponent in round-based combat → execute finishing move on victory → progress through tournament bracket
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Mastering character-specific move sets and combos while reading opponent patterns; managing risk/reward of aggressive vs defensive play
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Martial artist competing in supernatural tournament, earning the right to execute defeated opponents with signature finishing moves
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Emphasizes spectacle and character expression through multiple finishing moves (Fatalities, Babalities, Friendships, Stage Fatalities) over pure mechanical depth; graphic violence as core identity; faster, more combo-focused gameplay than predecessor signals shift toward execution mastery

difficulty.continues_system: Arcade-style continue system allowing players to resume after defeat
difficulty.checkpointing: Tournament bracket progression; each victory advances to next opponent; defeat requires continue or restart
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Character selection affects matchup difficulty; no explicit difficulty settings documented; multiplayer allows skill-based competition

progression.unlock_mechanism: Hidden characters (Noob Saibot) accessible through specific tournament paths or cheat codes; boss characters (Shao Kahn) fought at tournament conclusion
replayability_hooks: Multiple playable characters with distinct move sets
Non-canonical story endings for each character
Multiple finishing moves per character encouraging experimentation

world_building_techniques: Character-specific tournament endings establishing non-canonical story branches
Stage environments representing different Outworld realms (Pit II with stage-specific fatalities)
Character introductions expanding franchise mythology (Kitana, Mileena, Kung Lao, Noob Saibot added)
Plot continuation from original game establishing Shao Kahn as primary antagonist
Lore detail: reveals Sub-Zero lineage (Bi-Han killed by Scorpion, replaced by brother Kuai Liang)

boss_design.philosophy: Boss characters (Shao Kahn, Kintaro) serve as tournament finale representing apex difficulty; designed as pattern-based encounters requiring mastery of player character's moveset against superior opponents
notable_bosses: Shao Kahn (supreme ruler of Outworld, final tournament opponent)

faction_indicators: Earthrealm warriors (Liu Kang, Raiden's chosen fighters)
Shang Tsung's allegiance shift from sorcerer to Shao Kahn loyalist
danger_signals: Tournament held in hostile territory (Outworld) providing home field advantage to Shao Kahn
Shao Kahn's restoration of Shang Tsung as threat renewal
Boss characters exceeding normal fighter capabilities
Supernatural realm setting establishing otherworldly threat
environmental_storytelling: Pit II stage with background figure (Blaze, later retconned) and stage-specific execution mechanics
Multiple Outworld realm environments representing conquered kingdoms
Stage fatalities (abyss, spikes, acid pools) reinforcing inhospitable setting

sources_used: Wikipedia - Mortal Kombat II (gameplay mechanics, plot, character introductions, boss design, finishing moves system)
